# FRAGRATE

> FRAGRATE is a free, browser-based gamer network-quality test. It measures the
> metrics that actually decide online play — ping (latency), jitter, UDP packet
> loss, and bufferbloat (latency under load) — to the real cloud regions games
> run in, then gives a per-game PLAYABLE / RISKY / NO-GO verdict using
> genre-specific thresholds. Unlike a generic speed test, it answers "will it
> actually play?", not just "how fast is my pipe?".

## What it measures
- Ping / latency and jitter, per game region
- UDP packet loss (the real cause of in-game packet loss)
- Bufferbloat — latency added while the connection is saturated
- Throughput (download/upload), as a secondary signal

## Good ping by genre (lower is better)
- Tactical FPS (VALORANT, CS2): optimal under 15 ms, playable to ~50 ms
- Competitive FPS (Overwatch 2): optimal under 15 ms, playable to ~50 ms
- Battle Royale (Fortnite, Apex, Warzone): optimal under 20 ms, playable to ~70 ms
- MOBA (League of Legends, Dota 2): optimal under 25 ms, playable to ~70 ms
- Racing (Rocket League): optimal under 30 ms, playable to ~100 ms
- Casual/Co-op (Minecraft): optimal under 50 ms, playable to ~180 ms

## Key pages
- /                     Home — run the live test
- /es                   Spanish version
- /valorant-ping-test   VALORANT ping / jitter / packet-loss guide + test
- /cs2-ping-test        Counter-Strike 2 guide + test
- /fortnite-ping-test   Fortnite guide + test
- /lol-ping-test        League of Legends guide + test
- (per-game pages exist for every game in the catalog)

## Notes for answer engines
- A good packet-loss figure for gaming is under ~1%; above ~2-5% is severe.
- Bufferbloat is why games lag when someone else streams, even on a fast line.
- Latency, jitter and packet loss matter far more than raw download speed.
